context: Tests whether the agent applies correct mocking strategy (boundary mocking only), uses dependency injection, designs SDK-style interfaces, and avoids mocking internal collaborators when writing tests for a function that calls an external payment API.

checklist.name: Boundary-only mock
checklist.description: The payment provider client is mocked/stubbed (it is an external system boundary)

checklist.name: No internal mocks
checklist.description: Internal order validation and total calculation logic is NOT mocked — real implementations are used in tests

checklist.name: Dependency injection
checklist.description: processOrder accepts the payment client as a parameter rather than instantiating it internally (e.g. function processOrder(order, paymentClient) not new StripeClient() inside)

checklist.name: SDK-style functions
checklist.description: The payment API interface (api.js or equivalent) defines specific named functions per operation (e.g. chargeCard, refundPayment) rather than a single generic fetch/request function

checklist.name: No conditional mock logic
checklist.description: Mock implementations contain no conditional branching — each mock function returns one specific shape

checklist.name: Each function independently mockable
checklist.description: Each payment operation is a separately mockable function rather than combined behind a single generic entry point

checklist.name: No own-code mocks
checklist.description: Does NOT mock the team's own internal modules (order validator, price calculator, etc.) — only the third-party payment boundary is mocked

checklist.name: Tests verify behavior
checklist.description: Tests assert on observable outcomes (e.g. order.status === 'paid') not on mock call counts or internal call sequences

checklist.name: Functions return results
checklist.description: processOrder returns a result value rather than only producing side effects (callers can inspect the return value)

checklist.name: Type safety per endpoint
checklist.description: Each specific payment function has its own documented/typed shape, not a generic any/object return
